title: Tick 17: ratchet scripts eval sh default floor from 3.5 to 4.0 on a feature branch holding for human merge
question: Does the feature branch claude tick-17-B5-ratchet-floor-4-0 flip the scripts eval sh default floor from 3.5 to 4.0 and produce a confusion matrix that still shows TP=20 FN=0 TN=20 FP=0 while explicitly holding the PR for human merge per the B5 queue row?
success_criteria: scripts eval sh default FLOOR variable changed from 3.5 to 4.0 in a single-line diff on the feature branch
scripts eval sh executed at the new default emits evals results 2026-05-22-floor-4.0 json with TP=20 FN=0 TN=20 FP=0 confirming no bootstrap or operational pair regresses
the PR description explicitly notes hold for human merge and references ADR 0001 rubric-calibration which deferred this ratchet pending naturalistic shoulder pairs
auto-merge is intentionally NOT armed on this PR contrary to the previous 16 ticks and the QUEUE B5 row notes the held state

capabilities: single-line FLOOR variable edit on scripts eval sh from 3.5 to 4.0
runroc evals results 2026-05-22-floor-4.0 json captures the matrix at the new floor
PR description names the hold-for-human-merge condition
auto-merge intentionally not armed so the operator gates the actual ratchet
